title: Run Manifest
description: Every merge must produce a run manifest. This provides a complete audit trail enabling reproducibility and replay of any governance decision.

manifest_id.description: Unique identifier for this manifest. Format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<short-sha>.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this manifest was generated.

persona_set_commit.description: Git commit SHA of the .ai submodule (persona definitions) used during this run.

panel_graph_version.description: Version of the panel graph configuration (from project.yaml or governance config).

policy_profile_used.description: Name of the policy profile applied (e.g., 'default', 'fin_pii_high', 'infrastructure_critical').

model_version.description: LLM model identifier used for panel execution (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-6', 'gpt-4o').

aggregate_confidence.description: Weighted aggregate confidence score across all panels.

risk_level.description: Final assessed risk level after policy evaluation.

human_intervention.description: Record of human involvement in this decision.

reviewer.description: GitHub username of the human reviewer, if any.

override.description: Whether this was an enterprise override of a block decision.

override_justification.description: Required justification if override is true.

decision.description: The final governance decision for this merge.

rationale.description: Machine-generated rationale for the decision. Must reference specific policy rules.

panels_executed.description: References to panel output artifacts produced during this run.

artifact_path.description: Relative path to the panel output JSON artifact.

previous_manifest_id.description: ID of the previous manifest for this PR, if this is a re-evaluation.

escalation_chain.description: Ordered audit trail of escalation events during governance evaluation. Each entry records when an agent escalated a decision to a human role, and optionally captures the human's response.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the escalation was initiated.

source_agent.description: The agent or system component that initiated the escalation (e.g., 'policy-engine', 'code-manager', 'security-review').

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the escalation was triggered.

human_decision.description: The human reviewer's response to the escalation, if one has been recorded.

action.description: The action taken by the human reviewer.

justification.description: Required when action is 'override'. Explains why the human overrode the governance decision.

decision_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the human decision was recorded.

reviewer.description: GitHub username of the human reviewer.
